text.title: Cohort Implementation Checklist
text.summary: Ordered steps from data infrastructure setup through team adoption for shipping production-grade cohort analysis.

list.item: Validate events are consistently instrumented; signup_date is reliable and in canonical timezone
Choose analytics tool (Mixpanel/Amplitude) vs warehouse-native (BigQuery/Snowflake/Redshift)
Set up data warehouse if not present; configure user and event table schemas
Validate data accuracy: check for duplicate events, missing user_ids, timezone inconsistencies
Author dbt model in long-format: cohort_week, user_id, day_offset, active_users, cohort_size, retention_pct
Use incremental materialization with cohort_week partition and user_id cluster
Add dbt tests: not_null on cohort_week and user_id, accepted_values for day_offset, retention_pct between 0 and 100, cohort_size greater than 0
Validate warehouse model matches analytics-tool numbers to within 1pp before trusting dashboard
Set up automated daily refresh (dbt Cloud job, Airflow DAG, or Cloud Scheduler)
Create cohort split by acquisition channel
Create cohort split by signup cohort quarter
Create behavioral cohort (onboarding completed vs not)
Create feature-adoption cohort for each candidate retention driver
Connect long-format cohort table to BI tool (Metabase, Looker, Mode, Hex)
Build cohort heatmap with data-driven color scale (not hard-coded vmin/vmax)
Add D30 retention trend line per cohort week
Configure date-range selection and segment filters
Set up automated weekly email reports for retention review
Run dbt tests on every refresh; alert on failures
Monitor for event tracking errors (unexpected event_type values, schema drift)
Alert if any cohort_size drops below minimum sample size threshold
Train team on reading cohort heatmaps: time on x-axis, cohort on y-axis, color = retention pct
Document retention metric definition in dashboard description
Schedule weekly cohort review; assign action items from anomalies
Create action planning process: identify drop-off period, hypothesize cause, design experiment
